fields.name: Data Template Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the data template. When you create a new template, you can specify a code that contains up to ten characters, numbers, and spaces.

fields.name: Line No.
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of data in the data template.

fields.name: Field ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of the field that is part of the data template.

fields.name: Field Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the field in the data template.

fields.name: Table ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the table in the data template.

fields.name: Table Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the table that is referenced in the data template line.

fields.name: Template Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the data template.

fields.name: Template Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description for the template.

fields.name: Mandatory
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether it is required that there be data in the field in the data template. By default, the check box is selected to make a value mandatory. You can clear the check box.

fields.name: Reference
fields.tooltip: Specifies a url address. Use this field to provide a url address to a location that specifies additional information about the field in the data template. For example, you could provide the address that specifies information on setup considerations that the solution implementer should consider.

fields.name: Default Value
fields.tooltip: Specifies the default value with reference to the data template line.

fields.name: Table Caption
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the caption of the table on which the data template is based. The caption comes from the Caption property of the table.

You cannot change the contents of this field.

fields.name: Field Caption
fields.tooltip: Specifies the caption of the field on which the data template is based. The caption comes from the Caption property of the field.

fields.name: Skip Relation Check
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the relationship between the table fields should not be checked. This can useful when you want to specify a value for a field that is not yet available. For example, you may want to specify a value for a payment term that is not available in the table on which you are basing you configuration. You can specify that value, select the Skip Relation Check box, and then continue to apply data without error.
